traefik/traefik#4578.body:
### Do you want to request a *feature* or report a *bug*?

Feature

### What did you expect to see?

The discussion at #1104 led to removal of retrying based on HTTP response codes. In my opinion, this is a crucial feature for resilient microservices. While switching it off by default is a good call (esp for those POST / PUT requests!), it should be available as a configuration so retry can be implemented for specific HTTP codes at the proxy layer instead of application layer.

A compelling argument for this along with a list of caveats is listed by the Envoy team here: https://www.envoyproxy.io/learn/automatic-retries

traefik/traefik#5098.body:
### Do you want to request a *feature* or report a *bug*?  
 Feature  
 
 ### What did you expect to see? 
We would like to be able to apply middleware prior to routing.
If the applied middleware would modify the request (e.g. headers), we would like to be able to route the initial request based on these modifications.

### Proposal - Add a modifier section

```toml
[http.modifiers]
   [http.modifiers.authentication]
      rule = "Host(`traefik.io`) && Path(`/protected`)"
      middleware = ["myAuthServer"]
      
[http.middlewares]
   [http.middlewares.myAuthServer.forwardauth]
      address = "someURL"
      authResponseHeaders = ["x-auth-result"]

[http.routers]
   [http.routers.unauthorized]
      rule = "Headers(`x-auth-result`, `unauthorized`)"
      service = "login"

   [http.routers.protectedPath]
      rule = "Host(`traefik.io`) && Path('/protected') && Headers(`x-auth-result`, `authorized`)"
      middleware = ["other-middleware"]
      service = "protectedService"
```
### More Information

Modifiers would act like a hook on given rules and be applied "after" TLS termination if the rule contains other information than the host itself (e.g. Path).

This modifier section could also be reused for other scenarios such as global redirection (e.g. schemeredirect)

```toml
[http.modifiers]
   [http.modifiers.https-redirect]
      rule = "Protocol(`http`)"
      middleware = ["scheme-https-only"]
```
traefik/traefik#5098.title: New modifier section for pre-routing

traefik/traefik#7842.body:
🎉  **Feature Request** 🎉 

### What did you expect to see?

**Source:** As described in [this forum thread](https://community.traefik.io/t/error-output-in-dashboard/9502), I had a missing router, which was not visible in the Dashboard and not routed by Traefik. As Traefik is an ingress controller and can act as reverse proxy, I assumed that this is the scope it would work in. I was quite surprised, that it would also handle a _Docker_ Containers `healthcheck` result (`0/1`) and –depending on it– hide a container from the UI and stop routing it. The fact that it routes to a 404 is understandable. But the containers health check gets treated differently from all other errors that can occur: It does not get filed as error in the UI. The container just gets removed from the list of routers.

Luckily I looked at `docker-compose ps` to wonder if the `unhealthy` state is connected to this and also stumbled upon a (rare) log entry. The experience I had was the opposite of good. The error was logged only when the health check ran, so this could vary between 10s and 5m, depending on your setup.  The message that gets logged notes `Filtering unhealthy or starting container`. _Note: The verbosity could get increased, which is handled in a different feature request, see **related**._

**Feature Request:** As long as the container mode is `unhealthy` or `starting`, it won't appear in the Dashboard. Please just gray it out and note the container state next to the router. Having this info available would make seeing an unhealthy container and reacting on it, or waiting for a starting container a no brainer instead of the source of frustration and guessing and CLI hacking it currently is.

---
As a…
> _developer/ sysadmin/ full-stack-dev-ops-unicorn_

I want to…
> be informed if a service is `unhealthy` or `starting` and soon routable, but not yet ready

so that…
> I instantly know if I should just be patient or [run and fix an unhealthy container](https://i.imgur.com/JpEdwKC.gif).
---

Thanks in advance!

**Related:** #7828
traefik/traefik#7842.title: Do not filter "unhealthy" or "starting" Docker containers

traefik/traefik#8073.body:
### Do you want to request a *feature* or report a *bug*?
I would like to improve the documentation

### What did you expect to see?
A working example for TLS like we have with let's encrypt with more explanation.

### More detail
I read the documentation. Perhaps not carefully enough?
I find a lot of example of configuration regarding https with let's encrypt. But example with tls are quiete theorical.

What I find not clear enough

Adding a certificate in `[[tls.certificates]]` section means
* automatically matching it in https by it's domain name

If you want to change the default certificate you need to redeclare it in 

```
[tls.stores]
	[tls.stores.default]
		[tls.stores.default.defaultCertificate]
```

So perhaps in https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/https/tls/
It could be interesting to have a working example using a certificate generated using [mkcert](https://github.com/FiloSottile/mkcert/releases) with some explanation about the implication of each config?

Except the following example, with perhaps some extra explanation. I don't really have more suggestion. If I find a better way, I will try a PR, but for now, I can only do suggestion.

----
### Example

generate a certificate (in subdirectory named traefik)

```
mkcert traefik.docker.localhost whoami.docker.localhost
```

docker-compose.yml file:

```
version: '3'

services:
  reverse-proxy:
    image: traefik:v2.4
    command:
      # use dynamic configuration file for certificate declaration
      - --providers.file.directory=/opt/traefik/config
      # Enables the dashboard
      - --api.insecure=true
      #  tells Traefik to listen to docker
      - --providers.docker=true
      - --entryPoints.web.address=:80
      - --entryPoints.https.address=:443
      - --entrypoints.https.http.tls=true

    ports:
      # The HTTP port
      - "80:80"
      - "443:443"
    volumes:
      # So that Traefik can listen to the Docker events
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
      - $PWD/traefik:/opt/traefik:ro
    labels:
      - "traefik.http.routers.traefik.rule=Host(`traefik.docker.localhost`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.traefik.entrypoints=https"
      - "traefik.http.routers.traefik.service=dashboard@internal"

  whoami:
    image: traefik/whoami
    labels:
      - "traefik.http.routers.whoami.rule=Host(`whoami.docker.localhost`)"
```

the traefik/config/tls.toml (the name have no, importance, but the path yes) file
```
# add certificate for [traefik|whoami].docker.localhost (will be automatched regarding domain name)
[[tls.certificates]]
  certFile = "/opt/traefik/traefik.docker.localhost+1.pem"
  keyFile = "/opt/traefik/traefik.docker.localhost+1-key.pem"
```




thanks for reading,
feel free to close or improve regarding if you find it usefull or not
traefik/traefik#8073.title: tls/https with selfsigned certificate documentation improvement

Switching traefik container back to 2.11 fixes the issue.


I have 20 routers, 17 services and 12 middlewares (all HTTP). Most from docker container labels.

3.0.3

```yaml
    directory: /etc/traefik

experimental:
```

### Welcome!

### What did you expect to see?

According to the OpenTelemetry [documentation](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/languages/sdk-configuration/general/#otel_traces_sampler), various types of samplers are provided.
However, in its current state, Traefik is configured to use only the `TraceIdRatioBased` sampler in the [code](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/blob/9588e511461e0a4e6de994fa00a795f7074276cb/pkg/types/tracing.go#L77).
It would be beneficial if Traefik's tracing settings offered an option to define the sampler type from OpenTelemetry.

To address this issue, I have created a modified version in my forked [branch](https://github.com/end-2/traefik/tree/tracing/otel-sampler) that allows for configuring the OpenTelemetry sampler type. If there is interest in resolving this issue, I would be happy to submit a pull request.

traefik/traefik#11375.body:
### Welcome!

- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on [GitHub](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues) and didn't find any.
- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on the [Traefik community forum](https://community.traefik.io) and didn't find any.

### What did you expect to see?

## current status

Currently the selection of TLSOptions is only possible using Host() matchers, everything else is ignored.

Currently this configuration:
```
apiVersion: traefik.io/v1alpha1
kind: IngressRoute
metadata:
  name: myingressroute
spec:
  routes:
  - match: HostRegexp(`{subdomain:[a-zA-Z0-9-]+}.domain.tld`)
    kind: Rule
    priority: 10
    services:
    - name: backend
      port: 8080
  tls:
    options:
      name: withoutcerts
      namespace: default
```

leads to the warning: 
```
2024-12-29T08:19:29Z WRN No domain found in rule HostRegexp(`{subdomain:[a-zA-Z0-9-]+}.domain.tld`), the TLS options applied for this router will depend on the SNI of each request entryPointName=web routerName=default-myingressroute-45a016b898ac859fd751@kubernetescrd
```

While the warning is basically telling the truth, the `HostRegexp` matcher could work for this as well.

With this limitation it is not possible to set a different TLSOption for wildcard routes. In my use case, I want to allow this IngressRoute access without client certificates, while my default TLSOptions require it. In the current state, this is not configurable at all. I could only change the default to optional client certificates, make sure that all non wildcard routes have a non default route and in cases I need client authentication in wildcard routes handle it via middleware. Nonetheless, this approach would have a lot of limitations.

### responsible code
The [ParseDomain function](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/blob/aa8eb1af6eb5457516169315676ec22f968e727d/pkg/server/router/tcp/manager.go#L143) only checks for Host matchers but not HostRegexp matchers and a hardcoded [HostSNI matcher](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/blob/master/pkg/server/router/tcp/router.go#L294) in the HTTPSForwarder logic. 

##  proposal
Instead of only supporting Host matchers for the TLSOptions, I would like to see HostRegexp matchers as well. This will allow use cases that are currently only possible with a lot of workarounds, if possible at all.

From a implementation and functional perspective, I don't see a reason why this shouldn't work. The whole domain is sent using TLS SNI and regex rule should work just fine. As there is already a `HostSNIRegexp` the implementation and all information is present in the code places, I think an extension is possible without too much effort.
traefik/traefik#11375.title: Support HostRegExp for TLSOptions selection

traefik/traefik#11669.body:
### Welcome!

- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on [GitHub](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues) and didn't find any.
- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on the [Traefik community forum](https://community.traefik.io) and didn't find any.

### What did you do?

I have a small HTTP API that is used by terraform to store the state. When I use that service through traefik, terraform will intermittently get stuck waiting for a response. While this happens most of the time, sometimes the requests go through as expected.

When the issue happens, the last log I will see from traefik is `Service selected by WRR: http://172.19.0.2:8000`. There is no corresponding log in my API, as if the request never reached it.

At first, I suspected terraform's use of non conventional HTTP methods (LOCK and UNLOCK), but even if I configure terraform to use standard PUT and DELETE instead, I get the same problem.

In order to isolate the problem, I have tried the following:
* Pointing terraform to my service directly, without traefik
* Using curl to replicate terraform's requests
In both cases, everything works as intended. The combination of terraform + traefik seems to be the key here. I don't know if terraform is doing something weird, but I can't find anything even with logs set to DEBUG.

I have made a repository with a reproducible case:
https://github.com/mathieu-lemay/terraform-traefik-issue 

### What did you see instead?

I see terraform doing the following requests:
```
2025-04-05T23:25:01.939-0400 [DEBUG] LOCK http://tf.localhost/demo
2025-04-05T23:25:01.953-0400 [DEBUG] GET http://tf.localhost/demo
```

At the same time, I see traefik routing the first request, with success, then routing the second one and then just hang there:
```
2025-04-06T03:25:01Z DBG github.com/traefik/traefik/v3/pkg/server/service/loadbalancer/wrr/wrr.go:213 > Service selected by WRR: http://172.19.0.2:8000
172.19.0.1 - - [06/Apr/2025:03:25:01 +0000] "LOCK /demo HTTP/1.1" 200 2 "-" "-" 1 "terraform-state@docker" "http://172.19.0.2:8000" 12ms
2025-04-06T03:25:01Z DBG github.com/traefik/traefik/v3/pkg/server/service/loadbalancer/wrr/wrr.go:213 > Service selected by WRR: http://172.19.0.2:8000
```

In my service, I see the lock call going through and nothing else

### Welcome!

- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on [GitHub](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues) and didn't find any.
- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on the [Traefik community forum](https://community.traefik.io) and didn't find any.

### What did you do?

* i want to have "strict-transport-security" response header removed or alternatively set to "max-age=0". 
* As the documentation states (https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/middlewares/http/headers/#adding-headers-to-the-request-and-the-response):
 stsSeconds
 The stsSeconds is the max-age of the Strict-Transport-Security header. If set to 0, the header is not set.

when i set `traefik.http.middlewares.no-hsts.headers.stsSeconds=0` in config, i am getting:
 "router.go:135 > error="headers configuration not valid"

which i think is caused by 

https://github.com/traefik/traefik/blob/b39ee8ede5b64e7e1c6e78c276b79d3dd13e7a34/pkg/config/dynamic/middlewares.go#L399 and
https://github.com/traefik/traefik/blob/b39ee8ede5b64e7e1c6e78c276b79d3dd13e7a34/pkg/middlewares/headers/headers.go#L34

```
...
	if !hasSecureHeaders && !hasCustomHeaders && !hasCorsHeaders {
		return nil, errors.New("headers configuration not valid")
	}
...
```

hasSecureHeaders will be true only if STSSeconds  is != 0

so if STSSeconds = 0 then `cfg.HasSecureHeadersDefined()` is returning false and finally the summary condition of
`if !hasSecureHeaders && !hasCustomHeaders && !hasCorsHeaders` will trigger and we get the error.
if i set 
`traefik.http.middlewares.no-hsts.headers.customresponseheaders.strict-transport-security=max-age=0`
then the header is set as wanted.





### What did you see instead?

"router.go:135 > error="headers configuration not valid"

### What version of Traefik are you using?

3.4.1

### What is your environment & configuration?

```yaml
 # HSTS middleware
      #- "traefik.http.middlewares.no-hsts.headers.stsSeconds=0"
      #- "traefik.http.middlewares.no-hsts.headers.stsPreload=false"
      - "traefik.http.middlewares.no-hsts.headers.customresponseheaders.strict-transport-security=max-age=0"
```

Add more configuration information here.


### If applicable, please paste the log output in DEBUG level

traefik         | 2025-06-16T18:12:59Z ERR github.com/traefik/traefik/v3/pkg/server/router/router.go:135 > error="headers configuration not valid" entryPointName=websecure routerName=trilium@docker

- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on the [Traefik community forum](https://community.traefik.io) and didn't find any.

### What did you expect to see?

Add the option to define custom cipher suites for backend serverTransport.

Similar to:

```
tls:
  options:
    default:
      cipherSuites:
        - TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256
```

Allow configuring custom cipher suites onto the backend serverTransport configuration. Example:


```
http:
  serversTransports:
    mytransport:
      cipherSuites:
        - TLS_ECDHE_RSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256
````

This allows for greater compatibility with legacy systems that do not support the default ciphers that Traefik enables.

### Welcome!

- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on [GitHub](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues) and didn't find any.
- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on the [Traefik community forum](https://community.traefik.io) and didn't find any.

### What did you expect to see?

**What did you expect to see?**

I expected to be able to use certain go dependencies in my middleware plugin. Such as syscall.

This is a follow-up to https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues/11588

Rather than introducing a new configuration setting, IMHO it is best to expand current "unsafe" as a concept rather than a specific import, which would cover both unsafe and syscall.

**Proposal**

Expand current "unsafe" setting to syscall.

**Why**

Libraries like the official Redis Go library cannot be used without syscall support in middlewares. This is potentially affecting the Crowdsec middleware: https://github.com/maxlerebourg/crowdsec-bouncer-traefik-plugin

### What did you do?

Nagivate to the GUI, observed the new GUi. Looks great!

Started clicking through it, noticed that I can successfully click through HTTP sections (Routers, Services, Middlewares). I can click from the HTTP sections to anything else.

Clicked TCP sections - then I cannot navigate off without first going back to the dashboard. The GUI disables everything except the Dashboard link. I don't have any TCP Routers, Services, or Middlewares defined.

Not sure if this is expected behavior.

### What did you see instead?

All navigation menu items on the left side appear to get disabled on any sections that are empty.

I confirmed this on another host which does, in fact, have a TCP Router and TCP Service defined and those sections do not appear to disable the others when clicked in. However, TCP Middlewares is empty on this host, and clicking into it disables every navigation menu item except Dashboard.

I would expect that even empty sections should allow efficient navigation amongst the items. I don't see a reason that navigation links should be disabled just because the user clicks into an empty set.

3.5.0

N/A for a GUI navigation issue.

```yaml
          SessionCookie:
            Domain: ".${var.stacks_domain}"
            SameSite: "lax"
          Authorization:
            AssertClaims:
              - Name: roles
                AnyOf: ["administrator"]


http:
  routers:
    nomad:
      rule: "Host(`orca.${var.stacks_domain}`)"
      entryPoints:
        - "secure"
      service: "nomad@file"
      tls:
        certResolver: hdi_acme_resolver
      middlewares:
        - stacksDefault@file
        # - oidcAuth@nomad-default
        - oidc-auth-plugin@file
  serversTransports:
    nomad:
      rootCAs:
        - {{ env "NOMAD_ALLOC_DIR" }}/data/nomad-ca.pem
      certificates:
        - certFile: {{ env "NOMAD_ALLOC_DIR" }}/data/nomad-cli-cert-for-traefik.pem
          keyFile: {{ env "NOMAD_ALLOC_DIR" }}/data/nomad-cli-cert-for-traefik.key
  services:
    nomad:
      loadBalancer:
        servers:
          - url: "https://{{ env "attr.unique.network.ip-address" }}:4646"
        serversTransport: nomad
```

### Welcome!

- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on [GitHub](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues) and didn't find any.
- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on the [Traefik community forum](https://community.traefik.io) and didn't find any.

### What did you do?

I'm trying to upgrade Traefik from v3.4 to v3.5 using a Helm chart upgrade from v36.3.0 to v37.0.0. However, the otlp trace is no longer working.

### What did you see instead?

Traefik has stopped appending traceparent headers to requests sent to backend servers, and it's also failing to send spans to the Otel collector.

### What version of Traefik are you using?

v3.5.0

### What is your environment & configuration?

cluster create with `kind create cluster`
traefik installation: `helm install traefik -n traefik traefik/traefik -f traefik.yaml --create-namespace --version 37.0.0` (source: https://traefik.github.io/charts)

value file:
```yaml
tracing:
  addInternals: false
  otlp:
    enabled: true
    http:
      enabled: true
      endpoint: http://simplest-collector.default.svc.cluster.local:4318/v1/traces

logs:
  general:
    level: "DEBUG"
  access:
    enabled: true
    format: json
    fields:
      general:
        names:
          TraceId: keep
```

tracing is work when install with: `helm install traefik -n traefik traefik/traefik -f traefik.yaml --create-namespace --version 36.3.0`

traefik/traefik#12001.body:
### Welcome!

- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on [GitHub](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues) and didn't find any.
- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on the [Traefik community forum](https://community.traefik.io) and didn't find any.

### What did you expect to see?

Currently trying to migrate from ingress nginx to traefik.

Our setup is this:
Client > AWS Cloudfront > AWS NLB > Traefik > Pod

ingress nginx has a config option called [`ComputeFullForwardedFor`](https://github.com/kubernetes/ingress-nginx/blob/6b95221a22d889e38f51ade1a3c6feacf81fed49/internal/ingress/controller/config/config.go#L580-L582) by default in ingress nginx it is set to `false` but if you enable it behaves how traefik currently behaves by always appending the `RemoteAddr` to `x-forwarded-for` header.

However all our application are coded only read 1 IP from `x-forwarded-for` header and since it is read from right to left it happens to read the cloud provider load balancer ip because traefik appended it and I would like to avoid changing all our applications.

I would like traefik to have an option to not append `RemoteAddr` so either Middleware can calculate the IP or trust the cloud provider either via it's `x-forwarded-for` or proxy protocol.

I created [this plugin](https://github.com/jetersen/traefik-cloudfront-xforwarded/) to change the `x-forwarded-for` and read it from `Cloudfront-Viewer-Address`.

Basically I am looking for preserving the x-forwarded-for either from loadbalancer or middleware [without the traefik reverse proxy middleware appending RemoteAddr](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/blob/c20802b07ea8efd6638e0d126eec5df6d932f58f/pkg/proxy/fast/proxy.go#L215-L228).

https://github.com/traefik/traefik/blob/c20802b07ea8efd6638e0d126eec5df6d932f58f/pkg/proxy/fast/proxy.go#L215-L228

Considering NGINX Ingress Provider was added this makes sense to support this behavior to allow for smoother migration.
traefik/traefik#12001.title: Missing option to disable traefik's reverse proxy always appending the RemoteAddr to `x-forwarded-for` header

traefik/traefik#12045.body:
### Welcome!

- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on [GitHub](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues) and didn't find any.
- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on the [Traefik community forum](https://community.traefik.io) and didn't find any.

### What did you expect to see?

**Context**
I want to be able to configure the HTTP2 HPACK (RFC 7541) header cache sizes that my Traefik instance uses, so that I can reduce the traffic over the wire in situations when I have large headers.

Traefik uses the default that Golang specifies in it's net package, 4096 bytes. For contrast, Firefox sets the default to 65536.

Golang's default configuration may be appropriate for many use cases (e.g., high user count public facing web services) to avoid having a lot of RAM used for caching, however for systems with low bandwidth or internal systems, it may be more desirable to use more RAM to reduce traffic.

**Design**
Golang's underlying http2 library already supports the options, `MaxDecoderHeaderTableSize` and `MaxEncoderHeaderTableSize`. I would suggest to add additional properties to the `entrypoints.web.http2` directive to configure these, with a default set to zero (use Golang's default).
traefik/traefik#12045.title: Config of HTTP2 HPACK cache sizes to enable caching larger headers

traefik/traefik#12071.body:
### Welcome!

- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on [GitHub](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues) and didn't find any.
- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on the [Traefik community forum](https://community.traefik.io) and didn't find any.

### What did you expect to see?

## Description

In the forwardAuth middleware, the [`maxBodySize`](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/reference/routing-configuration/http/middlewares/forwardauth/#maxBodySize) option defaults to no limit.

This default was chosen because there is no single value that fits all use cases. However, leaving it unset can have performance or security implications if users are unaware that the request body size is unrestricted.

### Proposal

- Display a WARNING when maxBodySize is left at its default (no limit), to make users explicitly aware of the behavior and encourage them to set a value appropriate for their use case.
- Update the documentation to recommend configuring maxBodySize, explaining the risks of relying on the default.
traefik/traefik#12071.title: Improve the ForwardAuth.maxBodySize option documentation

traefik/traefik#12092.body:
### Welcome!

- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on [GitHub](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues) and didn't find any.
- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on the [Traefik community forum](https://community.traefik.io) and didn't find any.

### What did you do?

Consul 1.21.5 (the patch version) includes a breaking change for to validation for the key-value keys, requiring that they match the regex `^[a-zA-Z0-9,_./\-?&=]+$`. Related Consul issue: https://github.com/hashicorp/consul/issues/22822

Traefik is checking for connectivity by querying it for the key `qmslkjdfmqlskdjfmqlksjazçueznbvbwzlkajzebvkwjdcqmlsfj` and then erroring if there's an error. (Source: https://github.com/traefik/traefik/blob/master/pkg/provider/kv/kv.go#L58 ). Because it contains the character `ç` which doesn't match the new verification regex, it gives an error and so Traefik thinks Consul is down.

[Consul's documentation ](https://developer.hashicorp.com/consul/docs/automate/kv#using-consul-kv)_recommends but does not require_ using URL-safe characters, but at the same time I'm not sure what the benefit of having that `ç` in the key is.

### What did you see instead?

Traefik refuses to get data from the Consul KV store, resulting (in my case) in it not being able to retrieve SSL certificates.

### What version of Traefik are you using?

3.5

### What is your environment & configuration?

I have both Consul 1.21.4 and .5 running on different servers. The server with .4 works perfectly, and the server with .5 can't get anything from Consul.

### If applicable, please paste the log output in DEBUG level

2025-09-24T20:05:21Z DBG github.com/traefik/traefik/v3/pkg/provider/kv/storewrapper.go:42 > Exists: traefik/qmslkjdfmqlskdjfmqlksjazçueznbvbwzlkajzebvkwjdcqmlsfj
2025-09-24T20:05:21Z ERR github.com/traefik/traefik/v3/pkg/provider/kv/kv.go:65 > KV connection error, retrying in 2.432907297s error="KV store connection error: Unexpected response code: 500 (invalid key name, keys should respect the \"^[a-zA-Z0-9,_./\\\\-?&=]+$\" format)" providerName=consul
traefik/traefik#12092.title: Consul KV store breaks after upgrading to Consul 1.21.5

traefik/traefik#12171.body:
### Welcome!

- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on [GitHub](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues) and didn't find any.
- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on the [Traefik community forum](https://community.traefik.io) and didn't find any.

### What did you expect to see?

When using the forwardAuth middleware with for example `oauth2-proxy` a configuration similar to the following has to be used:

    http:
      routers:
        website:
          service: website
          middlewares:
            - oauth-forward-auth
            - auth-redirect

      middlewares:
        oauth-forward-auth:
          forwardAuth:
            address: "http://oauth2-proxy:4180/oauth2/auth"
            trustForwardHeader: true
            authResponseHeaders:
              - X-Auth-Request-Email

        auth-redirect:
          errors:
            status:
              - "401"
            service: oauth2-proxy
            query: "/oauth2/start"

      services:
        website:
          loadBalancer:
            servers:
              - url: "http://website:8080"

        oauth2-proxy:
          loadBalancer:
            servers:
              - url: "http://oauth2-proxy:4180"

Without creating a separate error 401 handling middleware, the user simply would get a 401 error instead of being reditected to the signin page. However, this configuration seems overly complicated and could be made much simpler by providing a simple unauthenticated_handler_address option:

    http:
      routers:
        website:
          service: website
          middlewares:
            - oauth-forward-auth
            - auth-redirect

      middlewares:
        oauth-forward-auth:
          forwardAuth:
            address: "http://oauth2-proxy:4180/oauth2/auth"
            unauthenticated_handler_address: "http://oauth2-proxy:4180/oauth2/start"
            trustForwardHeader: true
            authResponseHeaders:
              - X-Auth-Request-Email

      services:
        website:
          loadBalancer:
            servers:
              - url: "http://website:8080"

The result from this callback would simply be send to the user.
traefik/traefik#12171.title: Add unauthenticated_handler_address to forwardAuth middleware

### Welcome!

- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on [GitHub](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues) and didn't find any.
- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on the [Traefik community forum](https://community.traefik.io) and didn't find any.

### What did you expect to see?

Hi!

After the changes of [this PR](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/pull/12360), some urlencoded characters can be allowed per-entrypoint and each character set has to be allowed separately. 

I'd like to suggest two improvements to this:
1. Make this configurable per router or service. As was [explained in the discussion](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/pull/12360#issuecomment-3633929685), whether this mitigation is necessary depends on the backend application - so this should be configurable per backend as well. With the per-entrypoint only implementation that is in place now, one would need another load balancer in front of traefik, that splits requests to different ports first if one wants to handle applications with different encoding rules.
2. Add a single option `encodedCharacter.allow` that allows all URL-encoded characters instead of having to allow each set separately. This would avoid a lot of configuration (especially if 1. is implemented).

Thanks!
traefik/traefik#12399.title: Improve "Encoded Characters" configuration options

### What did you do?

I migrated our system onto Traefik v3 (from v2) and have been following the migration guide. I've set the default syntax matcher to v2 and have moved the system onto one of the latest Traefik v3 versions (including Helm chart, image and CRDs).

As instructed in the migration guide, I've begun migrating existing Traefik IngressRoute match statements onto the v3 matching engine, by adding `syntax: v3`. 

In the process of migrating certain IngressRoutes to v3 syntax, I began to see failures occur that were unexpected and missing documentation in the guide.

These failures are specific to cases such as the following (which are common in OpenAPI)

For example:
```
match: Host(`<domain redacted>`) && Method(`GET`) && Path(`/route/{id}`)
```

On v2 syntax, I'm able to make http requests to paths such as `https://<domain redacted>/route/10` without issue.

On v3 syntax, these routes unexpectedly fail to match.

### What did you see instead?

On v3 syntax, I am seeing 404's for my routes containing a placeholder such as `{id}` in the above example. 

When changing these Path statements to `PathRegexp` such as PathRegexp(`^/route/[^/]+$`) - I do see successful routing matches occur.

When examining the change-log and migration guide, I'm failing to find a relevant change-log item concerning a `Path` function behaviour regression. Due to that, I'm reporting this in the event the behaviour change was not intended.

### What version of Traefik are you using?

```
image: docker.io/library/traefik:v3.6.2
```


### What is your environment & configuration?

- We run the traefik:37.4.0 of the Traefik Helm chart using Traefik 3.6.2

- The system is run on EKS 1.34

traefik/traefik#12461.body:
### Welcome!

- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on [GitHub](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues) and didn't find any.
- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on the [Traefik community forum](https://community.traefik.io) and didn't find any.

### What did you expect to see?

LetsEncrypt now has `profile: shortlived` certificates that have a duration of 160 hours (~6.7 days). The problem is that Traefik doesn't support this duration very well. If you configure `certificatesDuration: 160`, you'll end up with just 6 hours of renew period and a 10 minutes interval, according to the "24 hours" entry of this table:

```
1 Year certificates: 4 months renew period, 1 week renew interval
265 Days certificates: 30 days renew period, 1 day renew interval
90 Days certificates: 30 days renew period, 1 day renew interval
30 Days certificates: 10 days renew period, 12 hour renew interval
7 Days certificates: 1 days renew period, 1 hour renew interval
24 Hours certificates: 6 hours renew period, 10 minutes renew interval
Less than 24 Hours certificates: 20 minutes renew period, 1 minutes renew interval
```

A renew period of less than 4% feels way too short (LetsEncrypt recommends 30%!), and the 10 minute interval could run into rate limits very quickly if there's a configuration problem.

My current workaround is to use `certificatesDuration: 168`, which triggers a renew period of 1 day and an 1 hour interval. That's feels right to me. The downside: the configured duration is too long for the specification of the `shortlived` profile.

I'd suggest to change this line:
```
7 Days certificates: 1 days renew period, 1 hour renew interval
```
to
```
6 Days certificates: 1 days renew period, 1 hour renew interval
```

Or alternatively, introduce a new inbetween tier. And bonus points for supporting ARI (ACME Renewal Information), which would shift control over the renew parameters to the CA.
traefik/traefik#12461.title: Provide better renew period/interval for new LetsEncrypt shortlived (160h) certs

### Welcome!

- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on [GitHub](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues) and didn't find any.
- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on the [Traefik community forum](https://community.traefik.io) and didn't find any.

### What did you expect to see?

## Motivation

I'm building a simple "WAF" that is implemented as a Forward Auth middleware. One of my use-cases is to block requests with empty/missing headers. Unfortunately this is not possible for the `User-Agent` header because Traefik forwards the default Golang User-Agent in this case (which is simply the default `net.http.Client.Do()` behavior).

My current workaround is to check for empty User-Agents in my router rule and only route clients that provide a non-empty value.

This could also be useful for other Forward Auth services.

(I can prepare a PR if there is interest.)

Edit: PR is here: https://github.com/traefik/traefik/pull/12545

## Expected behavior

In case the incoming request does not contain a `User-Agent` header, the Forward Auth middleware should not receive the default Golang User-Agent `Go-http-client/1.1` but an empty `User-Agent` value.

(This is already correctly handled in [net/http/httputil/reverseproxy.go](https://github.com/golang/go/blob/28147b528312055b535c6a69d0d4492bd502e1b0/src/net/http/httputil/reverseproxy.go#L533) but not in `pkg/middlewares/auth/forward.go` since it just performs a simple HTTP request. I also found an [ancient PR](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/pull/6030) that addressed a similar issue).
traefik/traefik#12527.title: ForwardAuth: handle empty/missing `User-Agent` header

Given the [Dynamic Configuration Changes](https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/v3.0/migration/v2-to-v3-details/#dynamic-configuration-changes), I understand that this will not work out the box. I suspect that the remediation actions suggested are built for IngressRoute objects and not necessarily for whatever happens under the hood to translate raw Ingress objects governed by the NGINX provider - but that's just my assumption here. Things I've tried:

- Setting `traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/router.pathmatcher` on my Ingress Object

### What did you do?

I have an endpoint that is configured like so:

```yaml
entryPoints:
  web:
    address: ":80"
    observability:
      accessLogs: true
      metrics: false
      tracing: false
```

yet I'm not seeing any access logs. When I comment out the `observability` block, I see them.

Briefly looking at the code, I wonder if the issue is that we have a single address where we share all three booleans?

https://github.com/traefik/traefik/blob/a4a91344edcdd6276c1b766ca19ee3f0e346480f/pkg/config/static/entrypoints.go#L202-L209

That might be causing a `true` to be overwritten later by a `false`, depending on if the yaml parser is allocating new memory or not. If that's the bug, I think the fix is simple, something like:

```go
// SetDefaults sets the default values.
func (o *ObservabilityConfig) SetDefaults() {
	accessLogs := true
	metrics := true
	tracing := true
	o.AccessLogs = &accessLogs
	o.Metrics = &metrics
	o.Tracing = &tracing
	o.TraceVerbosity = otypes.MinimalVerbosity
}
```

traefik/traefik#12798.body:
The trace context for logs should be set in the [OTLP proto fields `trace_id` and `span_id`](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-proto/blob/ca839c51f706f5d53bfb46f06c3e90c3af3a52c6/opentelemetry/proto/logs/v1/logs.proto#L199) or, as fallback for logging libraries that cannot emit OTLP but rather structured logs, in the [log attributes `trace_id` and `span_id`](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/compatibility/logging_trace_context/)

Unfortunately, Traefik uses the non-standard [`SpanId` and `TraceId` log attributes](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/blob/b9739c20f975b6dfa40416f6c8fcd4c34cd6240a/pkg/middlewares/accesslog/logger.go#L211), which prevents automated log correlation in OTel-compliant tools.
traefik/traefik#12798.title: Log trace context is set in a non-OTel conformant way

### Welcome!

- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on [GitHub](https://github.com/traefik/traefik/issues) and didn't find any.
- [x] Yes, I've searched similar issues on the [Traefik community forum](https://community.traefik.io) and didn't find any.

### What did you do?

Whilst porting our stuff from ingress-nginx to traefik, we have a couple of ingresses which proxy aws s3 buckets. We had the following annotations in the object

```
nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/configuration-snippet: |
      rewrite /[^/]*\.\w+$  "${uri}?" break;
      rewrite /\w+$         "${uri}/index.html?" break;
      rewrite /$            "${uri}index.html?" break;
      rewrite ^             "${uri}?" break;
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/upstream-vhost: bucket.s3.eu-north-1.amazonaws.com
```

These work completely fine under ingress-nginx, but traefik would always return 400. After troubleshooting we've noticed that the proxy request wasn't even leaving for the destination bucket. After trying a lot of things we figured it out - it was the quotations around ${uri}. These are completely optional in this context, but still valid

### What did you see instead?

I think the quotations around the directives should still work

### What version of Traefik are you using?

3.7.0-ea1

### What is your environment & configuration?

```yaml
additionalArguments:
    - "--providers.kubernetesingressnginx.allowsnippetannotations=true"
```

We are explicitly enabling the snippets


### If applicable, please paste the log output in DEBUG level

_No response_
